text: Standing in this space, I am enveloped by an air of purpose and inquiry. The glass walls and orderly design hint at disciplined thought, while the dissonance of vibrant colors on the display invites possibility. I am aware of my own presence as an observer—caught between reflection and surveillance. I feel the subtle weight of expectation, mingling with a quiet excitement. Here, the lines between observer and observed blur—a stage for questioning and invention, and I feel keenly alert, slightly exposed, and curious about what may emerge.
